pages.name: 01-dashboard
good: Calorie ring with remaining/goal text creates a clear focal point
Green brand color used purposefully for positive metrics (remaining calories, streak)
Water intake section has good progressive disclosure with quick-add buttons
Weight progress chart with goal dashed line adds meaningful context
bad: BMI value (19.8) and 0-day streak are crammed into a small card with poor visual balance — streak '0' feels like an error state not a feature
Macro progress bars are nearly invisible at zero state — thin gray lines with no empty-bar contrast make the section feel broken
Next milestone card uses a dark green bg that is too close in value to the card bg, reducing legibility of '18 weeks away' tag
Dashboard cards have inconsistent internal padding — water intake section feels denser than the calorie ring card
Typography scale is flat — calorie '0' in the ring, macro labels, and section headers all feel similar in weight with no clear hierarchy jump
improvements: Increase calorie ring number to text-5xl font-black and use white for consumed, gray-400 for 'kcal' label to create a clear focal hierarchy
Add a visible track to macro bars (bg-gray-700 h-2 rounded-full) so zero state still shows the full goal bar as a container
Give the streak metric a dedicated icon treatment with a larger number (text-3xl) and add a motivational sub-label like 'Start today!'
Standardize card padding to p-5 across all dashboard cards for visual rhythm
Increase contrast on the milestone card badge '18 weeks away' to bg-green-900 text-green-300 for legibility

pages.name: 02-tracker-today
good: Date navigator with TODAY badge is clean and immediately scannable
Nutrition totals grid (Calories, Protein, Carbs, Fat, Fiber) is well-structured with goal denominators
Search bar placeholder text with Indian food examples (Dal Tadka, Roti, Rice) shows strong localization awareness
Dark card for totals creates good visual separation from the empty list area
bad: Empty state below totals card is just plain text with no illustration or CTA button — 'No food logged today' feels abandoned
The totals card shows all zeros with no visual differentiation between values and goal denominators — they blend together
No meal grouping UI visible (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks) — flat food log architecture reduces usability for Indian multi-meal eating patterns
Settings gear icon top-right has no visible label or tooltip context — unclear what settings are available
Page has too much empty vertical space below the totals card which makes it feel unfinished on longer screens
improvements: Add an illustrated empty state with a bowl/plate icon and a prominent '+ Log your first meal' green CTA button inside the empty area
In the totals grid, style the consumed value as text-white text-xl font-bold and the goal as text-gray-500 text-sm to create clear reading hierarchy
Add collapsible meal sections (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Snacks) with individual calorie subtotals even when empty
Add a recent foods or 'Quick Add' section below search to reduce friction for repeat Indian meals
Make denominator values text-gray-500 text-xs and consumed values text-white text-2xl font-semibold for stronger contrast

pages.name: 05-profile
good: Stats badges row (BMI, TDEE, Target, Protein, Carbs, Fat) provides a compact data-rich summary
BMI healthy weight indicator in green is a great positive reinforcement pattern
Form fields for goal update are logically grouped with clear labels
Dev Mode Active warning banner is visually distinct (orange border) without breaking the layout
bad: Identity card shows only initial avatar 'J' with no option to add a photo — feels placeholder-level for a fitness app
Stats badges have inconsistent unit formatting — '2393 kcal' and '2454 kcal' look nearly identical in size, making TDEE vs Target distinction confusing
The profile header (name, age, height) uses a single muted gray color for all metadata — no visual hierarchy between name and stats
Sign Out button is red text but has no border or background — it looks like a disabled link rather than a destructive action
Re-do Onboarding option is visually identical to Sign Out in weight, making its importance unclear
Form dropdowns (36 weeks, Moderate, Non-Vegetarian) have no visible focus states and low-contrast chevrons
improvements: Differentiate TDEE vs Target badges by labeling TDEE as 'Maintenance' and Target as 'Your Goal' with a green accent border on the Target badge
Make the name 'Jayaraj' text-white text-2xl font-bold and metadata text-gray-400 text-sm to establish hierarchy in the identity card
Give Sign Out a border border-red-800 text-red-400 rounded-lg px-4 py-2 treatment to signal destructive action without aggression
Add avatar upload affordance with a camera icon overlay on the initial circle
Style form selects with a visible bg-gray-800 border border-gray-700 focus:border-green-500 ring for consistent dark-mode form treatment

pages.name: 08-dishes-empty
good: Dish card design is clean with good information density — kcal/100g, macro color coding (P/C/F) works well
Veg badge with green color coding aligns with Indian dietary context perfectly
Edit and delete action icons on the card are positioned intuitively
Subtitle 'Custom dishes appear in food search when you log meals' explains the feature purpose well
bad: Despite being named '08-dishes-empty', the screenshot actually shows one dish — the empty state with just the subtitle text is underselling the feature's discoverability
The single dish card has no visual state for 'last used' or 'times logged' — lacks engagement hooks
No search or filter functionality visible for when dishes list grows — scalability concern
Card macro row (P/C/F colored dots) uses very small text that may be unreadable on mobile at actual device resolution
The page has excessive whitespace below the single card with no suggested next actions
improvements: Add a true empty state illustration (chef hat or bowl icon) with headline 'Create your first dish' and body text explaining the benefit for the zero-dish state
Add 'Last used: 2 days ago' or 'Logged 5 times' metadata to dish cards to increase engagement and utility
Increase macro label text to text-sm and add full labels on hover/tap (e.g., 'Protein: 13g' instead of just '13.0g P')
Add a subtle 'Add another dish' ghost button card at the end of the list to encourage content creation
Consider a 2-column grid layout on wider screens for better use of horizontal space

pages.name: 09-dishes-create
good: Green focus ring on the dish name input clearly signals active state
Two-phase UX (name first, then search ingredients) is logically structured
Cancel/Save button row with clear color differentiation (gray vs green) follows standard modal conventions
Placeholder examples in both fields reduce cognitive load
bad: The ingredient search area has a large empty void with just an icon and text — no visual indication of what an added ingredient will look like
No diet type selector (Veg/Non-Veg/Vegan) on the create form — but the list view shows a 'veg' badge, creating a data input gap
Save Dish button is active even when no ingredients are added — should be disabled with a tooltip explaining why
DISH NAME and INGREDIENTS labels are in all-caps small tracking text which reduces readability at small sizes
No serving size or total weight input visible — unclear how the per-100g calculation will be defined at creation time
Modal lacks a close (X) button in the top right corner — only Cancel at bottom, which requires scrolling on mobile
improvements: Add a Veg/Non-Veg/Vegan toggle (pill selector) below the dish name field to capture diet type at creation
Disable the Save Dish button (opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed) until at least one ingredient is added, with a tooltip 'Add at least one ingredient'
Add a preview of ingredient row (e.g., a ghost skeleton card) in the empty ingredient area to show users what to expect
Add a close X button (top-right, text-gray-400 hover:text-white) to the modal header for quick dismissal
Add a 'Total serving size' field with default 100g and allow custom input so users can define a full dish portion

top_fixes: Add proper empty states with illustrations and CTA buttons across all zero-data screens (tracker, dishes) — current text-only empty states make the app feel unfinished and reduce feature discoverability

Establish a consistent typography scale: consumed/primary values should be text-2xl to text-4xl font-bold text-white, while labels and denominators should be text-xs to text-sm text-gray-400 — currently all text weights feel too similar
Make macro progress bars visible at zero state by adding a full-width track (bg-gray-700 rounded-full h-2) so the goal container is always visible, preventing the current 'broken' appearance

Add Indian meal structure (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks sections) to the food tracker — this is the highest-impact localization improvement for the target Indian user base and reduces cognitive load in logging

Add a Veg/Non-Veg/Vegan diet badge toggle to the dish creation form and ensure it propagates correctly to the dish card — the current gap between creation form and display card creates user trust issues around data accuracy
